question: What are the key order and load tables in the OP system and how do they relate to each other?
answer: Key order and load tables in the OP system (OPCIGP database, OP schema): ORDP100A = customer order header (order-level). ORDP120B = customer order detail / line items. LOAD_DEPART_OP1F = load departure records — contains LLR date (Load Label Release date) and load number. LOAD_CLOS_CNTRL_BC2C = load close control — also contains LLR date and load number, represents the closing/billing control. DIV_MSTR_DI1D = division master, holds DC/division metadata including fin_div_cd which maps to dc_id in WMSHUB. Relationship query: SELECT * FROM ordp100a a, ordp120b b, load_depart_op1f f, load_clos_cntrl_bc2c c, div_mstr_di1d d WHERE a.div_part = b.div_part AND d.div_part = a.div_part AND f.div_part = a.div_part AND c.div_part = a.div_part AND a.load_depart_sid = f.load_depart_sid AND c.llr_dt = f.llr_dt AND c.load_num = f.load_num AND a.ordnoa = b.ordnob. Order status codes in OP: O = Open, R = Released, A = Shipped.
